name: Faherty
summary: A six-year partnership with a family-founded apparel brand spanning two flagship redesigns and an ongoing growth program.
challenge: When Faherty came to us in 2019, they were a fast-growing DTC brand on a templated theme. The site couldn't keep up with the catalog growth, and the team had no internal commerce engineering capacity.
approach: We started with a flagship build, then transitioned into an ongoing partnership covering site care, experimentation, and continuous growth feature development. As the brand evolved, we ran a second flagship redesign in year four to reflect the new positioning.
outcome: The partnership now operates as an extension of Faherty's in-house team. Site care and experimentation run continuously; new growth features ship every quarter; the second redesign reset the brand's digital flagship for the next phase.

growthFeatures.name: Shoppable Lookbook
growthFeatures.desc: An editorial-meets-commerce experience that drove a step change in collection sell-through.

growthFeatures.name: Gift Concierge
growthFeatures.desc: A guided gifting flow built for Q4 holiday peaks.

growthFeatures.name: Fit Finder
growthFeatures.desc: A product recommendation tool addressing fit confusion across categories.

timeline.title: Initial Flagship Build
timeline.body: A ground-up rebuild of Faherty.com. New IA, new design system, new Shopify Plus implementation. The foundation everything since has built on.

timeline.title: Site Care + Experimentation Begins
timeline.body: Transitioned into ongoing partnership. Monthly site care covering theme maintenance, performance, and small UX improvements. CRO program running structured tests against the new foundation.

timeline.title: Shoppable Lookbook
timeline.body: First major growth feature build — an editorial format that wove product into seasonal storytelling. Drove a measurable lift in collection sell-through.

timeline.title: Gift Concierge
timeline.body: Holiday-season gifting flow. Built once, refined every Q4 since.

timeline.title: Second Flagship Redesign
timeline.body: After three years of growth, the brand had outgrown its original digital identity. A second flagship rebuild — new design language, expanded category architecture, refreshed PDP and PLP — set the stage for the next chapter.

timeline.title: Fit Finder
timeline.body: Product recommendation tool addressing fit confusion across denim, outerwear, and women's tops. Reduced returns in the launched categories.

timeline.title: Continuous Optimization
timeline.body: The current operating mode. Site care and experimentation run as standing programs. New growth features get scoped and shipped each quarter, prioritized by the e-commerce roadmap we co-author.

name: Halfdays
summary: A refined storefront experience for a women's outerwear brand redefining performance and style.
challenge: Halfdays had outgrown its original Shopify theme. The site looked dated relative to the brand's ambition, the navigation buried key collections, and mobile performance was hurting conversions during peak seasons.
approach: A focused storefront refresh — not a full rebuild. We redesigned the core user journey templates, restructured the navigation around how customers actually shop, and built a flexible landing page system the team could use without engineering involvement.
outcome: Conversion lift held through two seasonal cycles. The marketing team now ships campaign pages without a developer in the loop, which has changed the cadence of how they go to market.

growthFeatures.name: Search + Navigation Redesign
growthFeatures.desc: Reworked nav structure and predictive search to reduce taps-to-product on mobile.

growthFeatures.name: Editorial Landing Pages
growthFeatures.desc: A flexible landing page system for seasonal drops and brand storytelling.

name: Marion Parke
summary: A storefront and brand voice refresh for a luxury footwear label rooted in podiatric design.
challenge: Marion Parke's product is technical — a luxury shoe designed by a podiatric surgeon — but the storefront was reading as a typical fashion site. The brand voice felt generic and the design didn't communicate what made the product different.
approach: We paired storefront optimization with a verbal strategy engagement, rebuilding the brand's voice and tone alongside the visual experience. The redesigned PDP became the proof point — every product page now leads with the design story.
outcome: A storefront that finally matches the level of craft in the product. The team has clear voice guidelines for everything from PDP copy to email campaigns.

growthFeatures.name: Editorial PDP
growthFeatures.desc: A product page treatment that surfaces the brand's podiatric design story alongside conversion-critical content.
